narrative:
Mohamed Salah is a pivotal right winger for Egypt, holding an overall rating of 7.49/10. This impressive score places him among the top 2% of right wingers at this tournament. His rating range, fluctuating between 7.13 and 7.84, reflects consistent performance across matches.

Salah exhibits strong playmaking abilities at this World Cup, averaging 0.533 assists per 90 minutes and creating 4.26 key passes per 90 minutes. While his World Cup goals per 90 min stand at 0.266, his expected goals per 90 min at club level over the last two seasons was significantly higher at 0.672. Defensively, he contributes 0.266 tackles and 0.533 interceptions per 90 minutes.

Salah is undoubtedly a primary attacking outlet for his national team, with 42% of his overall rating attributed to his 338 minutes played in this World Cup. Scouts should continue to monitor his exceptional key passing and expected assist output of 0.206 per 90 minutes. Fans should watch for him to convert more opportunities, aligning his goal-scoring with his prolific club form.
